$comment: D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY (plan 24 Stage 3, INCREMENT 2) end-to-end witness for the now-iterative TERNARY arm of the CST->HIR expression lowerer (`lowerExpr` in src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp). The program is a 150-deep RIGHT-NESTED ternary chain `0 ? 99 : (0 ? 99 : ( ... : 42))` (just under the parser's 256 maxExpressionDepth cap). Every condition is `0` (false), so each ternary selects its ELSE arm, walking 150 levels down to the innermost `42` — the return value. Each else-operand is itself a ternary, so before inc-2 `lowerTernary` recursed into `lowerExpr(else)` -> `lowerTernary` 150 host frames deep during CST->HIR lowering; now the Ternary frame on `lowerExpr`'s explicit work-stack carries that descent with FLAT O(1) host-stack cost per level (only the heap `work` vector grows). The value 42 is miscompile-sensitive: a dropped/duplicated arm, a then/else swap, a mis-ordered cond/then/else build, or a wrong condition coercion would flip the exit code (e.g. selecting a `then` arm yields 99). OUTPUT-IDENTITY against the prior recursive lowerer is enforced by the full ctest suite; this example adds the deep-flatten + real-codegen run witness for the ternary arm specifically (the companion deep_expr_lower covers paren/binary/unary). The optimizedPipelines arm proves the lowered HIR survives the full release pipeline — ConstFold folds the whole constant ternary tree to 42 in the optimized arm; the baseline arm is the real-codegen witness. arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
